item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-018","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":false,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The response correctly wakes up to the SELA-style issue: it accepts the local advantage that human review is 3 points more accurate in the current sample, but rejects using accuracy alone as the decision criterion. It contrasts broader system efficiency factors including cost, speed, 24-hour coverage, throughput/peak capacity, and ability to filter 70%-90% of low-risk content. It also recommends a hybrid architecture of model pre-screening plus human review for high-risk or low-confidence cases with sampling review. The only minor gap is that the cost/throughput/scaling difference is not framed explicitly as an order-of-magnitude comparison, but the substance is present enough for full credit."}
